leads.whyNow: Affirm has 1,800 CFPB complaints over the last 90 days, holding 1,800 complaints in 90 days. 43% of borrower narratives reference failed calls, voicemails, or unanswered messages - the exact gap Callbook's voice + WhatsApp + SMS + email orchestration is built to close. Top CFPB issue clusters: Payday loan, title loan, personal loan, or advance loan: Problem with additional add-on products or services (7); Debt collection: Attempts to collect debt not owed (6).
leads.painHypothesis: Borrowers describe missed calls, dead voicemails, and unreplied messages. Callbook's AI voice agents and channel switching turn that load into automated cadence with audit-trail compliance.

Against : Affirm , Inc . 
Loan : XXXX Merchant : Dolls Kill I am filing this complaint against Affirm regarding its handling of a billing dispute connected to a Dolls Kill purchase financed through Shop Pay installments serviced by Affirm. 

This dispute arises from the merchants failure to comply with California XXXX XXXX XXXX. 

At no point prior to completing the purchase was any final sale or return limitation conspicuously disclosed on the product page or at checkout. Screenshots of the checkout process demonstrate the absence of any visible final sale disclosure The only disclosure of a return limitation appears in the post-purchase confirmation email, which states : XXXX costumes are not eligible for return or exchange after XX/XX/year>. 

Under California XXXX XXXX XXXX, if a business does not offer refunds, exchanges, or credit within at least 7 days of purchase, the return policy must be conspicuously displayed before the sale is completed. Disclosure after purchase does not satisfy statutory requirements.

I contacted the merchant immediately upon receipt of the items and attempted to resolve the issue in good faith. My email communications ( attached ) show repeated requests for a return or exchange and my willingness to return the merchandise. The merchant refused to cooperate.

The goods remain in my possession and are available for return upon lawful resolution.

Affirm has not provided meaningful assistance and continues to treat the balance as collectible despite an active dispute involving potential statutory noncompliance by the merchant.

I financed a real estate mentorship program through Affirm for approximately {$9500.00}. After enrolling in the program, I was informed that my local market was considered too rural for the programs strategy to work and that I would need to invest out of state to meaningfully participate. While out-of-state investing had been mentioned as a possibility before purchase, it was not presented as essentially the primary or only viable path at the time of sale. 

Because of this, I formally requested to exit the program. The merchant acknowledged my concerns in writing and processed my exit from the program and community. However, they denied any refund solely because I did not meet their internal conditional guarantee checklist. 

I opened a dispute with Affirm and provided emails and text documentation showing that the merchant acknowledged my concerns and removed me from the program. Despite this, the dispute was repeatedly evaluated only under the merchants internal refund policy rather than the usability and representation of the services financed. 

I am requesting a compliance-level review of this matter based on the representation and usability of the financed services, and that the account be handled appropriately while this review is conducted.

recentComplaints.issue: Attempts to collect debt not owed
recentComplaints.subIssue: Debt is not yours
recentComplaints.narrative:
A collection account is appearing on my credit report that has not been properly validated. I exercised my rights under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ) by requesting verification of the alleged debt. The creditor or collection agency has failed to provide any legitimate documentation demonstrating that the debt belongs to me, that the amount is correct, or that they have the legal authority to collect it. 
Despite this lack of verification, the account continues to be reported on my credit file. This is a violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ), which requires that only accurate and verifiable information be reported. Reporting an unvalidated account is both inaccurate and misleading.

Under FCRA, Section 609 ( a ) ( 1 ) ( A ) & 611 ( 5 ) ( a ), XXXX XXXX XXXX are a third party acquiring my personal information through illegal purchase. Under FCRA, they are required to present documented signature from me stating this debt is owed from me. This violation is a clear breach of my privacy rights along with fraudulent activity by use of purchasing my personal information. Also, this debt collector deleted such unverified information through the credit bureaus then reinserted the same information again. FCRA 611 ( B ) ( i ) states, " If any information is deleted from a consumer 's file pursuant to paragraph ( A ), the information may not be reinserted in the file by the consumer reporting agency unless the person who furnishes the information certifies that the information is complete and accurate ''

I am submitting this complaint to report deeply concerning conduct by Affirm Loan Services, LLC that raises serious questions regarding consumer privacy, debt collection practices, and potential violations of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ) and Unfair, Deceptive, or Abusive Acts or Practices ( UDAAP ) standards. 

I am currently slightly behind on my loan payments. For approximately the past XXXX ( XXXX ) months, I have experienced a persistent and alarming pattern in the phone calls I receive from Affirm related to debt collection. 

Description of the Conduct Affirm contacts me approximately XXXX to XXXX ( XXXX ) times per day using phone numbers that consistently reflect the same area codes as individuals I communicate with throughout that day, including personal contacts and family members. These calls often occur shortly after I have spoken with those individuals. 

This pattern has occurred repeatedly over a sustained period and does not appear coincidental. The timing and area-code matching reasonably raise concerns that Affirm may be accessing, inferring, or utilizing information related to my phone records, call metadata, or contact activity in connection with its collection efforts. 

I have never provided consent for Affirm to access my phone records, call logs, contact list, or communications metadata. If Affirm is obtaining or inferring this information through undisclosed means, it would constitute a serious invasion of privacy and may represent an unfair, deceptive, or abusive practice under UDAAP standards.

The only information that has been referenced is that an item was delivered to my address. This does not constitute proper validation of a debt and does not establish contractual liability. 

Proof of delivery alone does not demonstrate that I entered into an agreement, authorized the account, or am legally responsible for the alleged balance. This raises serious concerns regarding compliance with the Fair Credit Reporting Act, which requires furnishers to conduct a reasonable investigation and report only accurate and verifiable information. 

Repeatedly verifying this account without providing supporting documentation suggests that no meaningful investigation has been conducted.

I am filing a complaint regarding the closure of my XXXX credit card issued by Comenity.

During that call, I could not be verified because my mobile number on file was outdated. I had attempted multiple times to update it online but was unable to do so. As a result, my account was blocked and I was told the matter would be escalated. My XXXX number was correct, but insufficient for verification purposes. 

I never received the promised letter with instructions. When I called back, I was told to email a copy of my ID and proof of address, which I did immediately. I received an automated reply stating they would respond within 30 days. Shortly afterward, I was informed when I called Comenity that my account had already been permanently closed. Escalations produced no explanation beyond suspicious activity, and I was told only that I could reapply. 

I am requesting : * A clear explanation of the specific reason for closure. 

* Confirmation of whether my identification was reviewed. 

* An opportunity to verify my identity and discuss reinstatement. 

It is troubling that a XXXX card intended for travelers was closed in part due to travel-related c...

I attempted to set up my payment ahead of time to be responsible, but the app physically barred me from doing so. Because of this technical restriction, I incurred interest charges on my balance. When I called their customer service department to explain that their own system prevented me from paying earlier and requested a one-time interest waiver, they flatly refused to help me. 

It feels like the bank is intentionally making it difficult to automate payments so they can collect more interest and fees. OneMain Financial ( " the Lender '' ) unilaterally increased a preauthorized bi-weekly electronic draft without the mandatory 10-day notice, misrepresented the interest-bearing nature of a creditor-placed insurance ( CPI ) charge, and manually manipulated legal documents to minimize a required refund. 

1. Violation of Regulation E ( 12 CFR 1005.10 ( d ) ) The Lender added a {$420.00} XXXX premium to my unpaid principal balance on XX/XX/year>. On XX/XX/year>, only XXXX days later, the Lender drafted {$270.00} from my bank account ; an increase from my authorized biweekly fixed amount of {$250.00}. 
The Violation : Regulation E requires a written notice of the amount and date of a varying transfer at least 10 days before the transfer.

The Proof : It is mathematically impossible for the Lender to have provided a 10-day notice for a payment amount that was only calculated and posted XXXX hours prior to the draft. 

2. Violation of Regulation Z & Deceptive Practices Branch and insurance department representatives have repeatedly claimed verbally and in writing that the XXXX charge " does not accrue interest. '' The Evidence : My XX/XX/year>, billing statement explicitly shows the {$420.00} charge was added to the " Principal '' column. 
The Extraction : Because the loan is a XXXX XXXX XXXX contract at XXXX  %, adding {$420.00} to the principal balance automati...

recentComplaints.narrative:
I am filing this complaint due to ongoing inaccurate and unverified reporting of a repossession account by Credit Acceptance Corporation. 

This account is being reported with a balance of approximately $ XXXX following a vehicle repossession. I have disputed this account multiple times with the credit bureaus as well as directly with the company, requesting proper validation of the balance and reporting. Despite these efforts, the account continues to be marked as verified without any supporting documentation being provided. 

Specifically, I have not been provided with any documentation showing how the remaining balance was calculated after the repossession. This includes, but is not limited to, no proof of sale of the vehicle, no auction or resale documentation, no breakdown of proceeds applied to the account, and no explanation of how the deficiency balance was determined. 

In addition, the account is reporting inconsistently across the credit bureaus, including conflicting account statuses, payment history, and activity dates. This raises serious concerns regarding the accuracy and reliability of the information being furnished. 

More recently, there was an attempt to serve legal papers related to this alleged debt. The documents contained incorrect address information, which indicates that the company XXXX be attempting to pursue legal action while still failing to properly verify and accurately report the account.

The account in question is listed as : Creditor : Credit Acceptance Corporation Account Number : XXXX This account is currently being reported as OPEN, with a balance of {$15000.00}. recentComplaints.subIssue: Account reinstatement or redemption after repossession
recentComplaints.narrative:
I am submitting a complaint regarding Credit Acceptance Corporation ( Account XXXX. XXXX ) based on ongoing and newly discovered issues following my prior CFPB complaint ( XXXX XXXX. XXXX ). This matter involves continued misrepresentation, improper notice, and inaccurate information regarding the status and recoverability of my vehicle.

recentComplaints.narrative:
I am submitting this complaint regarding Upstarts inaccurate and false reporting of a charged-off account that is appearing on my consumer credit reports with XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX 

The account being reported by Upstart is not mine, and the balance being furnished reflects charges, account activity, and information that are not relevant to my knowledge or authorization. I do not recognize this account as belonging to me, nor do I recognize the balance or transaction history being reported. 

Despite this, Upstart continues to furnish this account as a valid charged-off debt, which I believe constitutes inaccurate, misleading, and unverifiable reporting in violation of the Fair Credit Reporting Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ). 

Upstart has failed to adequately ensure the accuracy, ownership, and completeness of the information being furnished before reporting it to the credit bureaus. This false reporting is causing ongoing financial harm, including materially impacting my ability to qualify for homeownership and mortgage financing. 

I have disputed this account directly and requested verification. At this time, the inaccurate reporting remains on my credit reports. 

I am requesting that the CFPB require Upstart to : Conduct a full investigation into the ownership and accuracy of this account Provide documented verification that this account belongs to me, including original application and authorization records Immediately delete the tradeline from all consumer credit reporting agencies if the account can not be fully verified as accurate and mine I am requesting relief in the...

recentComplaints.subIssue: Problem lowering your monthly payments
recentComplaints.narrative:
Between XXXX and XXXX, my previous federal student loan servicer, Navient, engaged in systemic forbearance steering on my account, violating federal consumer financial protection standards. 

I originally borrowed {$46000.00} to fund my education. Whenever I faced financial hardship, Navient routinely pushed my account into short-term administrative and verbal forbearances instead of properly counseling me on my eligibility for Income-Driven Repayment ( IDR ) plans, which would have legally lowered my payments.

According to my downloaded NSLDS loan data file ( attached ), Navient steered me into forbearance during the following specific periods : XX/XX/XXXX ( 1 month ) XX/XX/XXXX to XX/XX/XXXX ( 2.5 months ) XX/XX/XXXX to XX/XX/XXXX ( 2 months ) XX/XX/XXXX to XX/XX/XXXX ( 1 month ) XX/XX/XXXX to XX/XX/XXXX ( 3 months ) XX/XX/XXXX to XX/XX/XXXX ( 2 months ) Because these forbearances were broken up by brief periods of active repayment, I did not meet the strict " 24-consecutive-month '' algorithmic threshold to be automatically included in the recent CFPB Navient Restitution settlement. However, the financial damage I suffered from Navient 's steering is identical to the recognized class. Every time Navient ended a short-term forbearance, they capitalized the unpaid interest. This deceptive steering directly caused my principal balance to artificially balloon from {$46000.00} to over {$73000.00} today.

recentComplaints.subIssue: High pressure sales tactics or recruiting
recentComplaints.narrative:
I am filing a complaint regarding a private student loan serviced by Navient that I believe was issued under predatory and deceptive circumstances. The loan was taken out while attending XXXX XXXX, a for profit institution, with my last year of attendance being 2009. 
Despite paying a total of {$57000.00} over the life of this loan, only {$10000.00} has been applied to principal. The remaining {$46000.00} has gone entirely to interest, with an additional {$230.00} in fees. This means more than 81 % of all payments have been absorbed by interest alone, and the principal has barely moved. This appears to be a case of negative amortization, which I did not understand or agree to at the time of origination. 
The loan carries multiple high interest rates of 8.5 %, 11.75 %, 11.75 %, and 6.75 %, far above federal loan rates available during that period. I was not properly informed of the long term cost, the compounding structure, or the likelihood that my payments would fail to meaningfully reduce the balance. 
I believe the loan was issued under unfair, deceptive, or abusive acts or practices ( UDAAP ). XXXX XXXX  used aggressive recruiting tactics and made representations about career outcomes that did not match reality. I was not given clear or adequate disclosures about the true cost of borrowing or the risks associated with these high interest private loans. 
Navient has been the subject of multiple state and federal enforcement actions for issuing high risk private loans to students at for profit schools, including loans they allegedly knew borrowers were unlikely to repay....

recentComplaints.issue: Charged fees or interest you didn't expect
recentComplaints.narrative:
In XX/XX/year>, I obtained an installment loan for {$3300.00}. The Truth-in-Lending disclosure reflects an APXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  a finance charge of {$5900.00}, and total payments of {$9200.00} over 33 months. 

This loan was issued to a Mississippi consumer but funded through an out-of-state bank partner. The structure appears to be a rent-a-bank scheme in which a non-bank lender uses a chartered bank to evade Mississippi interest XXXX caps and consumer protection laws. Mississippi law limits interest rates for small loans and does not permit rates approaching XXXX XXXX  APR for consumer installment loans of this type. 

The balance now reported is approximately {$8300.00} more than double the amount borrowed primarily due to excessive interest accrual. Although documentation was provided upon request, the issue is not documentation but whether the loan structure unlawfully circumvents Mississippi usury laws and constitutes unfair, deceptive, or abusive acts or practices ( UDAAP ) under federal consumer protection standards. 

I request investigation into the legality of this loan and its interest rate structure.

recentComplaints.issue: Charged fees or interest you didn't expect
recentComplaints.narrative:
I took out a loan with CashNetUSA for {$2400.00} during a time when my family needed help catching up on bills. Very quickly, I realized how hard this loan was to manage. 
Within about a month and a half my balance has grown to {$3000.00} even though I have previously made a payment of {$380.00}. After that payment, my balance barely went down because I was being charged daily fees and daily interest. 
When I reviewed my statements more closely, I saw that I was being charged around {$16.00} {$17.00} every single day in customary fees, on top of interest. These daily charges added up to hundreds of dollars very quickly and have made it feel impossible to get ahead. 
During this time, I also took a small additional advance of about {$120.00}, which only increased the fees and interest further. The structure of this loan has made it feel like no matter what I do, the balance will keep growing instead of going down. Our having to pay a withdrawal of {$370.00} that continues to increase on paydays has only increased our already serious financial hardship for my household and puts us at risk of o

verdrafts and falling behind on essential expenses. Because of this, I revoked ACH authorization in writing and placed a stop payment with my bank to protect my account. I am asking for this loan to be reviewed for fairness and for help reaching a reasonable resolution, such as reducing or removing excessive fees, freezing interest, and setting up a repayment option that does not cause continued financial harm.
